title: Create Task data model and storage module
description: Implement the shared Task data model (id, title, done, createdAt) and tasks.json file I/O utilities used by all commands.
design:
## Context
All four command specs (add, list, done, delete) depend on a shared Task model and storage layer. This task creates the reusable foundation. Depends on the tracer bullet task for project structure.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Task model module exports: createTask(id, title) returning {id, title, done: false, createdAt: ISO string}
- [ ] loadTasks() reads tasks.json, returns [] if file doesn't exist
- [ ] saveTasks(tasks) writes JSON array to tasks.json
- [ ] Auto-increment ID helper: nextId(tasks) returns max(id) + 1 or 1 if empty
- [ ] No external dependencies — uses fs and path built-ins

## Files
- `store.js` (create) — project root, parent dir verified
- `model.js` (create) — project root, parent dir verified

## Patterns
- Use fs.readFileSync / fs.writeFileSync for simplicity (CLI is synchronous)
- JSON.parse / JSON.stringify with 2-space indent for human-readable storage
- tasks.json path resolved relative to process.cwd()

## Tests
- createTask(1, "Buy milk") returns correct shape with done=false and valid ISO date
- loadTasks() returns [] when tasks.json doesn't exist
- saveTasks([task]) then loadTasks() round-trips correctly
- nextId([]) returns 1; nextId([{id:3},{id:1}]) returns 4

## Verify
```bash
node -e "const m = require('./model'); console.log(m.createTask(1, 'test'))"
node -e "const s = require('./store'); console.log(s.loadTasks())"
```

title: Create package.json and CLI entry point with subcommand routing
description: Tracer bullet: Set up the project foundation with package.json (no external deps) and task.js entry point that parses subcommands and prints usage for unknown commands.
design:
## Context
This is the tracer bullet task — it establishes the entire CLI skeleton end-to-end: package.json → entry point → subcommand routing → output. All other specs (01-04) build on this foundation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] package.json exists with name, version, and bin field pointing to task.js
- [ ] task.js is the CLI entry point, executable via `node task.js <command>`
- [ ] Subcommands parsed from process.argv: add, list, done, delete
- [ ] Unknown subcommands print usage message listing all commands and exit with code 1
- [ ] `node task.js` with no args prints usage and exits with code 1
- [ ] No external dependencies — only Node.js built-ins

## Files
- `package.json` (create) — project root verified exists
- `task.js` (create) — project root verified exists

## Patterns
- Use process.argv[2] for subcommand routing
- Usage message format matches spec: `task add <title>`, `task list`, etc.

## Tests
- `node task.js` → prints usage, exits 1
- `node task.js unknown` → prints usage, exits 1
- `node task.js add` → no crash (handler stub can be empty for now)

## Verify
```bash
node task.js 2>&1; echo "exit: $?"
node task.js unknown 2>&1; echo "exit: $?"
```

title: project-setup: Scaffold Node.js CLI task tracker
add-task: Implement CLI command to add tasks
